title: Cranial Nerve Conditions
purpose: DBQs collect clinician-documented medical information VA may use when evaluating disability compensation claims.

diagnosis_current_condition: What condition are you claiming, and what diagnosis has a clinician given you?
When did symptoms begin, how have they progressed, and what treatment/medications do you use?
What records, tests, imaging, labs, or specialist visits support the diagnosis?
nexus_service_connection: What in-service event, injury, illness, exposure, or aggravation do you believe is connected?
When and where did it happen, what unit/job/exposure was involved, and what records or witnesses can support it?
If secondary, what service-connected condition caused or worsened this condition, and what medical evidence says so?
functional_impact_severity: How does the condition limit work, attendance, physical tasks, concentration, relationships, sleep, travel, or daily self-care?
What can you no longer do, do less often, or do only with breaks, medication, assistive devices, or help?
Give truthful examples using frequency, duration, severity, and flare-up details.

rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.frequency_duration: How often symptoms occur and how long they last, including daily baseline and episode/attack patterns.
rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.severity_scale: Best, typical, and worst severity using a clear scale when appropriate, such as pain 0-10.
rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.work_daily_life_impact: Concrete limits: sitting, standing, walking, lifting, sleep, missed work, concentration, hygiene, chores, driving, relationships.
rating_breakers_to_document_truthfully.flare_ups: How often flare-ups occur, triggers, duration, what gets worse, what you cannot do, and what helps.

diagnosis_current_condition: If there are additional diagnoses that pertain to cranial nerves, list using above format:
medical_history_onset_course_treatment: Describe the history (including etiology, onset and course) of the Veteran's cranial nerve condition (brief summary):
Cranial Nerve I (olfactory) (If checked, complete the Loss of Sense of Smell and Taste DBQ)
Cranial Nerves II - IV, VI (If checked, complete the Eye Conditions DBQ)
Cranial Nerve VIII (If checked, complete the Hearing Loss and Tinnitus DBQ)

G. Difficulty swallowing (If checked, indicate severity): Mild Moderate Severe
H. Difficulty speaking (If checked, indicate severity): Mild Moderate Severe
I. Increased salvation (If checked, indicate severity): Mild Moderate Severe
J. Decreased salvation (If checked, indicate severity): Mild Moderate Severe
K. Gastrointestinal symptoms (If checked, indicate severity): Mild Moderate Severe
L. Other symptoms (If checked, indicate severity): Mild Moderate Severe
Muscle strength testing (Rate strength using the following levels to estimate strength of muscle groups. This summary provides useful information for VA purposes)
A. Cranial nerve V: (Motor: muscles of mastication; clench jaw, palpate masseter, temporalis)
Right: Normal Mild Moderate Severe Complete paralysis
Left: Normal Mild Moderate Severe Complete paralysis
B. Cranial nerve VII, upper portion of face: (Motor: muscles of facial expression, shuts eyes tightly)
C. Cranial nerve VII, lower portion of face: (Motor: muscles of facial expression; grins)
D. Cranial nerve IX, X: (Motor: swallow, cough, palate elevation; "say ah", gag reflex if indicated)
E. Cranial nerve XI: (Motor: trapezius, sternocleidomastoid; shoulder shrug, turn head against resistance)
F. Cranial nerve XII: (Motor: protrude tongue, move tongue from side to side)
Comments, if any:

Right: Normal Mild Moderate Severe Complete paralysis

Indicate the cranial nerve(s) affected. For each nerve, indicate severity ("degree of paralysis"), basing the responses on symptoms and findings from the above
exam. This section provides an estimation of the severity of the Veteran's cranial nerve condition, which is useful for VA purposes.
NOTE: For VA purposes, the term "incomplete paralysis" indicates a degree of lost or impaired function substantially less than the description of
complete paralysis that is given below with each nerve, whether due to a varied level of the nerve lesion or to partial regeneration.
Right: Not affected Incomplete,
Incomplete, severe Complete
Left: Not affected Incomplete,

functional_impact_prompts: Note: Provide the impact of only the diagnosed condition(s), without consideration of the impact of other medical conditions or factors, such as age.
Regardless of the Veteran's current employment status, do the conditions listed in the diagnosis section impact his/her ability to perform any type of occupational
task (such as standing, walking, lifting, sitting, etc.)?

sections.title: SECTION I - DIAGNOSIS
items: NOTE: Disabilities from conditions involving cranial nerves I, II, III, IV, VI, and VII are addressed in other questionnaires, including Eye, Hearing Loss and Tinnitus,
and Loss of Smell and Taste; if those cranial nerves are involved, the appropriate questionnaire(s) should be completed in addition or lieu of this Questionnaire.
If there are additional diagnoses that pertain to cranial nerves, list using above format:

sections.title: SECTION II - MEDICAL HISTORY
items: Describe the history (including etiology, onset and course) of the Veteran's cranial nerve condition (brief summary):
Cranial Nerve I (olfactory) (If checked, complete the Loss of Sense of Smell and Taste DBQ)
Cranial Nerves II - IV, VI (If checked, complete the Eye Conditions DBQ)
Cranial Nerve VIII (If checked, complete the Hearing Loss and Tinnitus DBQ)

recommended_user_inputs: Claimed condition and formal diagnosis, if known
Onset date/timeframe and service event, exposure, injury, or secondary condition theory
Treatment history, medications, devices, surgeries, hospitalizations, therapy, labs, imaging, or tests
Baseline symptom frequency, duration, and severity
Flare-up frequency, triggers, duration, severity, and functional loss
Work limitations, missed work, accommodations, daily activity limits, relationship/social effects
cheat_sheet_use.truthfulness_guardrail: Use this only to organize accurate personal facts. Do not exaggerate symptoms, invent diagnoses, or state medical causation unless supported by a qualified medical opinion.

script_templates.truthfulness_guardrail: Fill in only what is true. Skip wording that does not apply. Do not exaggerate, invent symptoms, stressors, exposures, test results, or medical nexus opinions.

default_options.duration: hours at a time, with worse episodes lasting the rest of the day
default_options.triggers: chewing, talking, cold air, touch, fatigue, stress, or flare-ups
default_options.limitation: eating, speaking, facial expression, vision/eye comfort, concentration, and work communication
default_options.treatment: neurology care, prescribed medication, therapy, assistive devices if prescribed, pacing, and rest
default_options.flareCare: rest, cooling, medication, reducing activity, and using support or assistive devices if prescribed
default_options.serviceEvent: [specific service event, injury, exposure, aggravation, or secondary condition]
default_options.workImpact: Symptoms slow my work pace, increase mistakes, create safety concerns, and require breaks or task changes.
default_options.dailyImpact: I have to pace activities, avoid overexertion, and may need help with driving, chores, or detailed tasks.
default_options.mainSymptoms: facial pain or numbness, weakness, eye or mouth symptoms, speech/swallowing issues, taste changes, and sensory changes if true
default_options.evidence: service records, treatment records, VA/private records, medications, imaging/labs/testing, profiles, buddy statements, or lay statements if true

sections.title: Medical History / Onset & Nexus
sections.intent: Answer when/where symptoms started, what service event/exposure/aggravation may connect them, and what treatment has been tried.
sections.dbq_wording_to_hit: current neurological diagnosis/residuals, onset, course, testing, treatment, and current symptoms; in-service event, injury, illness, hazardous exposure, aggravation, or secondary service connection timeline; neurology care, medications, therapy, assistive devices, testing, and specialist follow-up
sections.what_to_say_template: My claimed condition is {{currentDiagnosis}}. It first started or became noticeable {{onset}} after {{serviceEvent}}. Since then, the condition has continued with {{mainSymptoms}}. I have tried {{treatment}}, but symptoms still happen {{frequency}}. Evidence that may support the timeline includes {{evidence}}.

default_options.treatment: neurology care, prescribed medication, therapy, assistive devices if prescribed, pacing, and rest

sections.title: Symptom Evaluation
sections.intent: Answer DBQ-style symptom questions with exact frequency, duration, severity, triggers, and condition-specific symptoms.
sections.dbq_wording_to_hit: weakness, sensory changes, balance/coordination problems, fatigue, cognitive effects, safety concerns, and work/daily activity limits
sections.what_to_say_template: My symptoms are {{mainSymptoms}}. They happen {{frequency}}, usually last {{duration}}, and are triggered or worsened by {{triggers}}. On better days they are about {{bestPain}}; on worst days they reach {{worstPain}}. For example, symptoms occur {{frequency}}, often worsen with {{triggers}}, and can affect safety or task completion for {{duration}}.

default_options.limitation: eating, speaking, facial expression, vision/eye comfort, concentration, and work communication

subsections.title: Frequency & Duration
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Document frequency, duration, pattern, triggers, and whether symptoms are constant, intermittent, recurrent, or episodic.
subsections.what_to_say_template: My {{currentDiagnosis}} symptoms are {{frequency}}. They usually last {{duration}}. The symptoms I need documented are {{mainSymptoms}}. They get worse with {{triggers}}.
subsections.example_template: For example, symptoms occur {{frequency}}, often worsen with {{triggers}}, and can affect safety or task completion for {{duration}}.

subsections.title: Severity Scale
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Describe severity using measurable words, worst-day impairment, and any DBQ-specific severe-episode language that truthfully applies.
subsections.what_to_say_template: On better days, severity is about {{bestPain}}. On worst days, it reaches {{worstPain}}. Overall, I would describe it as {{severity}} because it interferes with {{limitation}}.
subsections.example_template: On a bad day, symptoms reach {{worstPain}} and I have to stop or reduce {{limitation}} instead of pushing through.

sections.title: Functional Impact / Occupational Assessment
sections.intent: Answer how the disability limits work, daily activities, relationships/social function, reliability, and worst-day functioning.
sections.dbq_wording_to_hit: weakness, sensory changes, balance/coordination problems, fatigue, cognitive effects, safety concerns, and work/daily activity limits

subsections.title: Daily Activities
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Explain work limitations, daily activity limits, missed time, accommodations, safety concerns, and reduced reliability/productivity.
subsections.what_to_say_template: This affects work because {{workImpact}} It affects daily life because {{dailyImpact}} I can usually continue for about {{limitBeforeStopping}} before I need to stop, rest, modify the task, or get help.
subsections.example_template: A real-life example is: when symptoms build, I slow down, make more mistakes, avoid unsafe tasks, and need breaks or support with {{limitation}}.

subsections.title: Flare-Ups / Worst Days
subsections.dbq_wording_to_hit: Describe flare-up frequency, triggers, duration, worst-day functional loss, and what helps relieve symptoms.
subsections.what_to_say_template: Flare-ups or worst episodes happen {{flareUps}}. They are triggered by {{triggers}} and can last {{duration}}. During them, I cannot keep up with {{limitation}}, and I use {{flareCare}}.
subsections.example_template: During a flare-up, weakness, sensory changes, fatigue, or balance/cognitive symptoms worsen, and I need {{flareCare}} before I can resume normal activity.
